title: Resource APIs, Pagination, Idempotency, and Concurrency Control · Go Semantics Case: Resource Schema Boundary
summary: A list endpoint skips and duplicates rows during concurrent writes, create retries duplicate charges, PATCH silently overwrites fields, and response schemas expose storage columns. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo resource schema boundary increment in stable resource and mutation consistency contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define stable transport schemas, domain models, persistence records, optional and null semantics, field ownership, and evolution boundaries separately.
Handle canceled requests and ambiguous commit results with idempotency, reconciliation, durable identifiers, and no claim unsupported by storage evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Predict Resource Schema Boundary · Theory Resource Schema Boundary
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 7607 in a bursting intake service near its concurrency ceiling. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate optimistic version conflicts from valid idempotency replay; then make specification and runtime disagree on one nullable field and block release. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
